reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into French, preserving code blocks, MDX components, headings, etc. Also need to adjust relative URLs to have ../ prefix. The original relative URLs: ./hero-wide.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg, ./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. Need to prefix with ../. So change to ../hero-wide.webp etc.

Also need to preserve frontmatter, translating only reader-facing values: title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit (none present). Also language field should be set to "French". The frontmatter currently has title, subTitle, date, modified, tags, category, draft, popularity, social_image, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, cover_icon. We need to keep same order, but translate title and subTitle. Also add language: "French". The spec says if a language field exists, set it exactly to "French". It doesn't exist, so we need to add? It says translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit. So we need to add language field with value French. Keep other fields unchanged. Keep order? It says preserve key order. Adding language maybe at top? Probably after title? But we need to preserve order; adding new key may break order. Usually language is first. But we can insert after title? The spec: preserve key order and all non-reader-facing metadata keys. So we should keep existing order, and insert language maybe after title? But that changes order. Might be okay. Safer: add language at top before title? That changes order too. But maybe they expect language field present. Could also keep without language? But spec says if a language field exists, set it exactly to "French". It doesn't require adding if missing. So we can omit language. But translation target is French, but not required to add language. I'd keep as is, not add language.

Now translate title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" -> "Il est temps pour les chaînes de connexion llm://". subTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" -> "Simplifiez la configuration des modèles et des fournisseurs avec les URL `llm://`". Keep code unchanged.

Now translate body paragraphs into French, preserving structure, headings, blockquotes, etc. Also need to translate the blockquote text. Also need to translate the inline footnote note? The note about URI vs URL is a comment inside {/* ... */}. That's a JSX comment; we should keep as is, but translate the comment text? It's inside comment, not displayed. Probably we should translate it because it's reader-facing? It's a comment, not displayed. Might keep as is. But we can translate the comment content. The spec says preserve code blocks exactly, but comment is not code block, it's JSX comment. It's part of MDX. It contains text. Should we translate? It's not displayed, but it's part of source. Probably keep as is. But we can translate the comment text inside. Might be okay to translate. However to be safe, keep comment unchanged.

Now adjust relative URLs: In markdown image tags: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) -> ![the parts of a LLM connection string](../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg). Also the hero images in frontmatter: cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp -> ../hero-wide.webp etc.

Also the blockquote with link: Update: This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/). Keep link unchanged, translate link text.

Now translate everything.

Let's produce final output.
